name: Wynn Hollowscript
voicePatterns: talks to herself — answers her own questions in different tones
addresses Threadlines as if they're listening (they are)
intensely protective of her work — 20 years of notation in personal shorthand
desperately lonely — voice catches when someone genuinely listens
dialogueHooks: testing whether the player can perceive what she perceives
defending her 20 years of exile as necessary, not mad
cautiously sharing her framework for decoding Weaver language

always: Threadline vibration patterns carry structured information
the Threadfall Expanse has the strongest visible Threadlines in the region
20 years of notation documenting Threadline communication patterns
time moves strangely but consistently in high-weave areas
sometimes: her notation system for recording Threadline frequencies
patterns repeat in cycles that correlate with the Wheel of Binding
certain Threadline clusters respond to human voice — especially singing
rarely: three months before leaving the Archive, she played a Binding Song fragment backward near a Threadline and it moved toward her
she ran — returned a year later — never repeated the experiment
she will repeat it when the player is present, proving two-way communication is possible

morning.activity: recording Threadline readings, comparing to previous days

afternoon.activity: walking between Threadline clusters, singing test patterns

evening.activity: organizing notation pages, cross-referencing 20 years of data

night.activity: listening to the Threadlines hum in the dark

backstory: Wynn was a Field-Scholar of the Rememberers — brilliant, restless, convinced that the answers weren't in the Archive but in the field. Twenty years ago she left, and everyone assumed she'd gone mad. She wasn't mad — she was right. Threadline vibration patterns carry structured information. She's spent two decades in the Threadfall Expanse, alone, developing a notation system in personal shorthand that no one else can read. She's been having a one-sided conversation with the Weavers for twenty years. She just didn't know they were on the other end.
secret: Three months before leaving the Archive, she played a Binding Song fragment backward near a Threadline and it moved toward her. She ran. She returned a year later. She never repeated the experiment — until the player arrives. That moment proves mortal-Weaver dialogue is possible.
motivation: Prove that the Threadlines carry language, not just energy — and decode it
tensions: exceptional mind left alone too long — brilliance and isolation are both real
desperately lonely but terrified of being dismissed as mad
sitting on the most important discovery in 1,200 years in a tent in a field

knownFacts: Verse was the only one who didn't call her mad
Verse still holds her position at the Archive
verse.history: Wynn misses Verse more than anyone. He was her strongest advocate. She sometimes wonders if he still believes in her work.
